g.text: Credential Lifecycle - Revoke / Suspend / Reactivate (Issuer-initiated, Bitstring Status List)
Status List is hosted by Core at /ssi/revocation/v1/list/{id}
Each credential -> statusListIndex (bit) in that list
reason (e.g. lost / data error / consent withdrawn)
2. Backend authorizes and validates state transition
permission to manage this credential type / organisation
[ERROR AUDIT] Store lifecycle failure audit log
Invalid state transition or insufficient permission
3. Backend calls Procivis Core to change status
Set status bit = REVOKED at statusListIndex
Set status bit = SUSPENDED at statusListIndex
Clear status bit = VALID at statusListIndex
4. Core publishes the updated Status List (serialize -> sign -> host -> verify)
bitstring -> gzip + base64 (reflecting the new bit)
[SPEC] Status List publication (same mechanism as Lifecycle-2):
Served by Core at the SAME public URL /ssi/revocation/v1/list/{id}
No new URL - verifiers always fetch the same list
No PII - only opaque bit positions
Verifiers fetch on-demand during VP verification (cacheable)
[ERROR AUDIT] Store lifecycle failure audit log
5. Backend persists status and writes audit log
Do not change holder key / reference
Next VP verification fetches Status List
-> credential shows REVOKED / SUSPENDED -> verification FAILS
-> VALID (after reactivate) -> verification can pass again
If SUSPEND had suspendEndDate, a scheduler reactivates at that time
triggers this REVOKE flow when consent is the only lawful basis
